name: eza
description: A modern, maintained replacement for ls

options.description: Show list of command-line options
name: --help

options.description: Show version of eza
name: -v
--version

options.description: Display one entry per line
name: --oneline

options.description: Display extended file metadata as a table
name: -l
--long

options.description: Display entries as a grid (default)
name: -G
--grid

options.description: Sort the grid across, rather than downwards
name: -x
--across

options.description: Recurse into directories
name: -R
--recurse

options.description: Recurse into directories as a tree
name: -T
--tree

options.description: Dereference symbolic links when displaying information
name: -X
--dereference

options.description: Display type indicator by file names
name: -F
--classify

options.description: When to use terminal colours (always, auto, never)
name: --color
--colour

options.description: Highlight levels of 'field' distinctly
name: --color-scale
--colour-scale

args.name: field
args.description: Comma-separated list of all, age, size

options.description: Use gradient or fixed colors in --color-scale (fixed, gradient)
name: --color-scale-mode
--colour-scale-mode

options.description: When to display icons (always, auto, never)
options.name: --icons

options.description: Don't quote file names with spaces
options.name: --no-quotes

options.description: When to display entries as hyperlinks (always, auto, never)
options.name: --hyperlink

options.description: Display entries with their absolute path (on, follow, off)
options.name: --absolute

options.description: Drill down into symbolic links that point to directories
options.name: --follow-symlinks

options.description: Set screen width in columns
name: -w
--width

options.description: Show hidden and 'dot' files. Use this twice to also show the '.' and '..' directories
name: -a
--all

options.description: Equivalent to --all; included for compatibility with `ls -A`
name: -A
--almost-all

options.description: List directories as files; don't list their contents
name: -d
--treat-dirs-as-files

options.description: List only directories
name: -D
--only-dirs

options.description: List only files
name: -f
--only-files

options.description: Explicitly show symbolic links (for use with --only-dirs | --only-files)
options.name: --show-symlinks

options.description: Do not show symbolic links
options.name: --no-symlinks

options.description: Limit the depth of recursion
name: -L
--level

options.description: Reverse the sort order
name: -r
--reverse

options.description: Which field to sort by
name: -s
--sort

suggestions.name: age
suggestions.description: Sort by file modified time (newest first)

suggestions.name: new
suggestions.description: Sort by file modified time (newest first)

suggestions.name: newest
suggestions.description: Sort by file modified time (newest first)

suggestions.name: none
suggestions.description: Do not sort files at all

options.description: List directories before other files
options.name: --group-directories-first

options.description: List directories after other files
options.name: --group-directories-last

options.description: Glob patterns (pipe-separated) of files to ignore
name: -I
--ignore-glob

options.description: Ignore files mentioned in '.gitignore'
options.name: --git-ignore

options.description: List file sizes with binary prefixes
name: -b
--binary

options.description: List file sizes in bytes, without any prefixes
name: -B
--bytes

options.description: List each file's group
name: -g
--group

options.description: Only show group if it has a different name from owner
options.name: --smart-group

options.description: Add a header row to each column
name: -h
--header

options.description: List each file's number of hard links
name: -H
--links

options.description: List each file's inode number
name: -i
--inode

options.description: Show mount details (Linux and Mac only)
name: -M
--mounts

options.description: List numeric user and group IDs
name: -n
--numeric

options.description: List file flags (Mac, BSD, and Windows only)
name: -O
--flags

options.description: Show size of allocated file system blocks
name: -S
--blocksize

options.description: Which timestamp field to list (modified, accessed, created)
name: -t
--time

options.description: Use the modified timestamp field
name: -m
--modified

options.description: Use the accessed timestamp field
name: -u
--accessed

options.description: Use the created timestamp field
name: -U
--created

options.description: Use the changed timestamp field
options.name: --changed

options.description: How to format timestamps (default, iso, long-iso, full-iso, relative, or a custom style '+<FORMAT>')
options.name: --time-style

args.name: style
args.description: A built-in style or a custom style like '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M'

options.description: Show the size of a directory as the size of all files and directories inside (unix only)
options.name: --total-size

options.description: List each file's permission in octal format
name: -o
--octal-permissions

options.description: Suppress the permissions field
options.name: --no-permissions

options.description: Suppress the filesize field
options.name: --no-filesize

options.description: Suppress the user field
options.name: --no-user

options.description: Suppress the time field
options.name: --no-time

options.description: Read file names from stdin, one per line or other separator specified in environment
options.name: --stdin

options.description: List each file's Git status, if tracked or ignored
options.name: --git

options.description: Suppress Git status (always overrides --git, --git-repos, --git-repos-no-status)
options.name: --no-git

options.description: List root of git-tree status
options.name: --git-repos

options.description: List each git-repos branch name (much faster)
options.name: --git-repos-no-status

options.description: List each file's extended attributes and sizes
name: --extended

options.description: List each file's security context
name: -Z
--context
